title: Race-blocked plus chronological-gap OOF calibration stack
summary: This task has two leakage axes: same-race dependence and temporal drift across years/rules/tyre choices.
rationale: This task has two leakage axes: same-race dependence and temporal drift across years/rules/tyre choices. GroupKFold handles non-overlapping races, while TimeSeriesSplit’s `gap` concept motivates an embargo between earlier and later race blocks. After that, OOF calibration should be fold-local; sklearn notes isotonic is stronger with enough samples, while sigmoid is safer on smaller subsets.
implementation_hint: Build OOF predictions with splits that never mix a race across train/valid and preserve approximate chronology across `Year` and race order, leaving a small embargo gap around validation race blocks if you use history-derived race statistics. Then calibrate only on OOF predictions: isotonic for the large dry-race subset, sigmoid for smaller wet/intermediate subsets, and average calibrated experts in the final blend.
expected_effect: More honest local CV and better public-score transfer, plus cleaner probabilities for ensembling heterogeneous experts.
risk: Stricter splits usually reduce local CV at first; if chronology is approximated poorly from the available columns, you can over-penalize training data without gaining leaderboard robustness.
